name: Saint-Pierre and Miquelon (Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon)
summary: Saint-Pierre and Miquelon is a North Atlantic civil-law French overseas collectivity (Collectivité d'outre-mer / COM) — structurally distinctive globally as the only remaining French jurisdiction in continental North America following the 1763 Treaty of Paris cession of New France to Britain and the 1803 Louisiana Purchase. Located off the coast of Newfoundland, Canada (~25 km from Newfoundland), it represents the last vestige of New France in North America. Family-law framework operates under the French Civil Code with COM-specific adaptations under the Organic Law 2007-223 framework. Parental authority (autorité parentale) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 371-387. The Court of Appeal of Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon (Tribunal Supérieur d'Appel) is the apex domestic appellate court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the French Court of Cassation in Paris. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Tribunal de Première Instance de Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon. Cross-border family-law coordination with neighboring Canada operates through standard France-Canada arrangements. Psychology profession is regulated through the French Republic professional framework as applicable in Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon. Saint-Pierre and Miquelon is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the French interest-of-the-child standard. Saint-Pierre and Miquelon is a Hague Convention 1980 party via French Republic territorial extension effective 1 December 1983.

statutory_framework.citation: French Civil Code arts. 371-387 (applicable in Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon)
statutory_framework.title: French Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: French Civil Code applicable in Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: COM Statut framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Organic Law framework for French overseas collectivities including Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon's COM status.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon (Tribunal Supérieur d'Appel)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon.

apex_courts.name: French Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon.

professional_regulators.name: French Republic professional framework (applicable in Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon)
professional_regulators.role: French professional regulatory framework for health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology applicable in Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon.

anonymisation_convention: Saint-Pierrais family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Appeal practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Samuel-de-Champlain-naming + early-French-claim framework
key_developments.description: Samuel de Champlain named Saint-Pierre 1604 — substantively distinctive North Atlantic early-French-claim framework. Subsequent intermittent French-British control through 1763 Treaty of Paris affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Paris + retention-by-France-+-last-vestige-of-New-France framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Paris 10 February 1763 ended Seven Years' War — substantively distinctive globally most-of-New-France-ceded-to-Britain-with-Saint-Pierre-and-Miquelon-retained-by-France framework. Foundational substantive 262+ year last-remaining-French-jurisdiction-in-continental-North-America framework persisting through 21st century.

key_developments.title: Louisiana Purchase + structural-distinctiveness-reinforced framework
key_developments.description: Louisiana Purchase 30 April 1803 — substantively distinctive North Atlantic French-territorial-presence-in-North-America-reduction framework. Saint-Pierre and Miquelon's distinctive position reinforced as the only remaining French jurisdiction in continental North America. Foundational substantive constitutional-administrative-distinctiveness framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Code civil des Français + Napoleon-Code framework
key_developments.description: Code civil des Français (later Code Napoléon) 21 March 1804 applicable in Saint-Pierre and Miquelon — substantively significant North Atlantic French-Civil-Code-substantive-heritage framework. Foundational substantive civil-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation including arts. 371-387 governing autorité parentale and child custody.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Paris 1814 + return-of-Saint-Pierre-and-Miquelon-to-France-after-Napoleonic-occupation framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Paris 30 May 1814 + Saint-Pierre and Miquelon returned to France 22 June 1816 following 1793-1816 British occupation during Napoleonic Wars — substantively distinctive North Atlantic French-retention-after-British-occupation framework. Foundational substantive constitutional-administrative framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: US Prohibition + smuggling-economy framework
key_developments.description: US Prohibition 1920-1933 substantively transformed Saint-Pierre and Miquelon into smuggling-base for Canadian-and-French-alcohol-to-USA — substantively distinctive North Atlantic Prohibition-era-smuggling-economy framework with Al Capone-related operations. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation transformation affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Department status + transition framework
key_developments.description: Saint-Pierre and Miquelon achieved departmental status (DOM) 19 July 1976 — substantively distinctive North Atlantic département-d'outre-mer framework. Subsequent 1985 Territorial Collectivity transition + 2003 COM-status under Organic Law framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension + France-UNCRC-ratification framework
key_developments.description: Hague Convention 1980 territorial extension by France to Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon effective 1 December 1983. France ratified UNCRC 7 August 1990 with applicability to Saint-Pierre and Miquelon — establishing interest-of-the-child substantive doctrine within French-derivative-civil-law framework applicable to Saint-Pierre and Miquelon.

key_developments.title: Organic Law 2007-223 + COM-statut-framework + Assemblée framework
key_developments.description: Organic Law 2007-223 of 21 February 2007 establishing modern Collectivité d'outre-mer (COM) statut framework applicable to Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon — substantively significant North Atlantic COM-Assemblée framework. Foundational substantive COM framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Conseil territorial framework + Canada cross-border framework + EU OCT status
key_developments.description: Conseil territorial de Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon framework continuing through 2023 under COM framework. Canada cross-border family-law adjudication framework substantively significant institutional context (~25 km from Newfoundland). EU OCT (Overseas Country and Territory) status framework substantively significant cross-border family-law adjudication context. Substantively significant North Atlantic French COM institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Tribunal Supérieur d'Appel de Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon + Cour de Cassation — interest-of-the-child substantive register + France-Canada-cross-border context
key_developments.description: Court of Appeal of Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon (Tribunal Supérieur d'Appel) and French Court of Cassation continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + Organic Law 2007-223 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within France-Canada-cross-border context (~25 km from Newfoundland). Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Saint-Pierre and Miquelon operates a French-civil-law framework with COM autonomy — places Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon in the French overseas collectivity cluster.
Only remaining French jurisdiction in continental North America is structurally distinctive globally — last vestige of New France following 1763 Treaty of Paris and 1803 Louisiana Purchase.
Geographic proximity to Canada (~25 km from Newfoundland) creates structurally distinctive France-Canada cross-border family-law coordination requirements.
Hague Convention 1980 applicability via French Republic territorial extension reflects COM Hague jurisdiction status.
editorial_notes: Saint-Pierre and Miquelon jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Champlain-naming-1604-to-France-Canada-cross-border-context trajectory: 1604-Samuel-de-Champlain-naming + 1763-Treaty-of-Paris-+-retention-by-France + 1803-Louisiana-Purchase + 1804-Code-civil-des-Français + 1816-Treaty-of-Paris-1814-+-return-after-Napoleonic-occupation + 1920-US-Prohibition-+-smuggling-economy + 1976-department-status + 1983-Hague-Convention-+-France-UNCRC + 2007-Organic-Law-2007-223-COM-statut + 2024-Tribunal-Supérieur-d'Appel-+-Cour-de-Cassation-interest.

Civil-law North Atlantic French COM (French Civil Code + Organic Law 2007-223 COM Statut framework + last vestige of New France 262+ years + ~25 km from Newfoundland + Hague via French Republic territorial extension 1983). Only remaining French jurisdiction in continental North America globally + US Prohibition smuggling-economy 1920-1933 distinctive.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 371-387 + Organic Law 2007-223 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins North-Atlantic + civil-law + French-COM-cluster + only-French-jurisdiction-continental-North-America-globally-distinctive-262-years + last-vestige-of-New-France-1763-Treaty-of-Paris-globally-distinctive + Champlain-naming-1604 + Louisiana-Purchase-1803-distinctiveness-reinforced + Code-civil-des-Français-1804 + Treaty-of-Paris-1814-+-return-after-Napoleonic-British-occupation-1793-1816 + US-Prohibition-1920-1933-smuggling-economy-Al-Capone-distinctive + department-status-1976-+-Territorial-Collectivity-1985-+-COM-2003 + Organic-Law-2007-223-COM-statut + France-Canada-cross-border-25km-from-Newfoundland + Hague-via-French-Republic-territorial-extension clusters within the corpus.
